codeReview.summary: The dashboard-polish feature adds accessibility improvements (role='alert' on error banner, aria-label on retry button), data-testid attributes for testability, and expands the test suite from 9 to 20 comprehensive tests covering all VAL-DASH assertions. The implementation is clean, follows project conventions (CSS variables, shared components, responsive grid), and all tests pass. The worker verified the page at 3 viewports (375px, 768px, 1440px) with real backend data and dark mode.

issues.description: data-testid='stat-card' is hardcoded on the shared StatCard component. This means every StatCard instance on every page will have the same test ID. While this works fine for the dashboard (where only 4 exist), it could be problematic if tests need to distinguish between StatCards on other pages (e.g., Finance, Support). Consider making the test ID configurable via props or using a more specific data-testid on the dashboard page wrapper instead.

The empty state when highlights is empty (but no error) renders nothing where stat cards would be — just falls through to null. Per AGENTS.md, pages should use the shared EmptyState component. While the quick-access section still renders, having no visual indicator that there are no dashboard metrics could confuse users. A subtle empty message like 'No metrics available' with the shared EmptyState component would be more polished.

The 'displays today's date' test only checks that the date element contains the current year. This is a weak assertion — it would pass even if the date format was wrong or only showed the year. A stronger assertion would check for the expected date string format (e.g., containing month name and day). However, this is a minor concern since the date rendering was verified manually via agent-browser.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend GET /portal/dashboard currently returns only 3 highlight metrics (Revenue Run Rate, Fulfilment SLA, Dealer Coverage) instead of 4. VAL-DASH-002 expects exactly 4 stat cards. The frontend correctly caps at 4 and renders whatever the API provides. This backend data discrepancy is noted in the handoff's discoveredIssues but is not documented in .factory/library/. Future workers or validators might spend time investigating this mismatch.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff discoveredIssues: 'Pre-existing: Backend returns 3 highlight metrics instead of 4 for GET /portal/dashboard.' Handoff interactiveChecks at 1440px: '3 stat cards in row (Revenue Run Rate ₹1180.00, Fulfilment SLA 0.0%, Dealer Coverage 2)'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The data-testid pattern for testability is emerging as a convention but is not documented in AGENTS.md. The worker added data-testid to StatCard, skeletons, and date display. This is a good pattern but should be documented as a project convention so all workers use it consistently.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: StatCard.tsx:21 added data-testid='stat-card', AdminDashboardPage.tsx:76 added data-testid='dashboard-date', AdminDashboardPage.tsx:92 added data-testid='stat-skeleton'. AGENTS.md has no section on data-testid conventions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker feedback suggests the frontend-worker skill's MANDATORY requirement to invoke frontend-skill adds unnecessary overhead for polish tasks where the UI already exists. This is a reasonable suggestion — the skill procedure should distinguish between 'new UI creation' (where frontend-skill is essential) and 'polish/existing UI refinement' (where it's less valuable).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The frontend-worker skill mentions invoking frontend-skill as MANDATORY, but for polish tasks where the page UI already exists and just needs accessibility/test improvements, it adds unnecessary overhead.'

summary: PASS — The dashboard-polish feature is well-implemented. The worker added proper accessibility attributes (role='alert', aria-label), data-testid attributes for testability, and expanded test coverage from 9 to 20 tests covering all VAL-DASH assertions. All tests pass. The implementation follows project conventions: uses shared StatCard component, CSS variables for colors, responsive grid (grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4), and proper loading/error states. Minor non-blocking issues: (1) hardcoded data-testid on shared StatCard may conflict across pages, (2) empty highlights state renders nothing rather than using shared EmptyState component, (3) date assertion in tests is weak. The worker verified the dashboard at all 3 breakpoints with real backend data and dark mode.
